title: Closing of the Daivasika Pratikramaṇa (Utthāpanā & Colophon)
summary: This closing step dismisses the temporary guru-installation (sthāpanā) made for the rite — if one was set up on a book or the like — with one Navkāra, and then declares that the daily (Daivasika) pratikramaṇa of the Vidhipaksha (Anchala) Gaccha śrāvaka is complete.

segments.recitation: Eka Navakāra gaṇī, utthāpanā vidhi karavī
segments.plainMeaning: If you set up a stand-in for the guru (on a book or the like) at the start, face it, recite one Navkāra, and formally take it down.
segments.idiomaticTranslation: If you had established a sthāpanā (a symbolic seating of the guru's presence) using a book or some similar object, then turn to face it, hold your right hand turned outward in the raising posture (mudrā), recite one Navkāra, and perform the utthāpanā — the rite of respectfully dismissing that installed presence.
segments.explanation: A procedural cue performed only when an improvised sthāpanā was used; the single Navkāra accompanies the closing hand-gesture as the symbolic presence is withdrawn.

ritualTerms.gloss: the symbolic installation of the guru's presence for the rite
the universal Namaskāra mantra to the five Parameṣṭhins
the closing rite of withdrawing/dismissing the installed sthāpanā

segments.recitation: Sthāpanācāryajī hoya to utthāpanā karavānī jarūra nathī
segments.plainMeaning: If the guru's proper sthāpanācārya is present, you do not need to do the dismissal.
segments.idiomaticTranslation: If the guru-reverend's own sthāpanācārya (the permanent consecrated stand representing the ācārya) is present, then there is no need to perform the utthāpanā at all.
segments.explanation: An exception to the previous segment: the dismissal applies only to an improvised installation, not to a permanent sthāpanācārya.

ritualTerms.gloss: the permanent consecrated object representing the ācārya/guru
the closing rite of withdrawing the installed sthāpanā

segments.recitation: Iti śrī vidhipakṣa (acala) gacchīya śrāvakasya daivasika pratikramaṇaṁ samāptaṁ ॥
segments.plainMeaning: Thus the lay devotee's daily pratikramaṇa of the Vidhipaksha (Anchala) Gaccha is finished.
segments.idiomaticTranslation: Thus concludes the Daivasika (daily, at-sunset) pratikramaṇa of the śrāvaka of the illustrious Vidhipaksha (Achala) Gaccha. Herewith it is complete.
segments.explanation: The Sanskrit colophon that formally seals the Devasi rite; 'acala' is given as the gaccha's alternate name.

ritualTerms.gloss: this tradition's self-name; also called Anchalgachchha
the daily sunset rite of reflection and atonement

notes: All three segments are procedural directions; none is a chanted verse, so the 'recitation' fields transliterate the key spoken cue (segment 01 references reciting one Navkāra; segment 03 is itself the spoken Sanskrit colophon). The right-hand-reversed raising mudrā in segment 01 is rendered descriptively from the native parenthetical (ઉઠાવવાની મુદ્રામાં); the precise technical name of this mudrā is not given in the KB and is left undescribed beyond the text. The utthāpanā/sthāpanā distinction follows the native wording and the KB's treatment of guru-sthāpanā (panchindiya); not independently re-verified against an external source.
